title: Excessive admin collaborators
description: More than 3 users have direct admin access to this repository, which increases the blast radius of a compromised account and makes access auditing harder.
recommendation: Review admin access and apply the principle of least privilege. Consider using teams with limited permissions instead of direct admin grants.

title: Direct collaborators instead of teams
description: The repository has direct collaborator access grants. Direct collaborator access is harder to audit and manage compared to team-based access.
recommendation: Prefer team-based access over direct collaborators for better auditability and easier access management.

title: Deploy keys with write access
description: One or more deploy keys have write access to this repository. Write deploy keys allow the key holder to push code, which poses a risk if the key is compromised.
recommendation: Use read-only deploy keys where possible. For write operations, prefer GitHub Apps or OIDC federation, which provide better auditability and can be rotated more easily.

title: Unverified deploy keys
description: One or more deploy keys are unverified. Unverified keys may not belong to authorized systems.
recommendation: Verify all deploy keys to ensure they are associated with authorized systems and remove any unknown keys.

title: Deploy keys present — consider GitHub Apps or OIDC
description: Deploy keys are configured for this repository. While functional, deploy keys are less auditable and harder to rotate than modern alternatives.
recommendation: Consider migrating to GitHub Apps or OIDC federation for automated deployment credentials, which offer better security, auditability, and key rotation.

title: No branch protection configured on default branch
description: The default branch has no branch protection rule, meaning anyone with write access can push directly, force-push, or delete it.
recommendation: CRITICAL: Enable comprehensive branch protection on production branches to prevent direct pushes, require code review, and enforce CI checks.

title: No approving reviews required before merge
description: The branch protection rule requires zero approving reviews, allowing pull requests to be merged without any peer review.
recommendation: CRITICAL: Require at least 1 approving review before merge to enforce code review and catch errors or malicious changes.

title: Only 1 approving review required
description: The branch protection rule requires only 1 approving review, which may be insufficient for high-risk changes on production branches.
recommendation: Consider requiring 2 or more reviews for production branches to reduce the risk of unreviewed code reaching production.

title: Stale reviews not dismissed on new commits
description: When new commits are pushed to a pull request, existing approvals are not automatically dismissed, so stale approvals remain valid even after the code changes.
recommendation: Enable stale review dismissal so that existing approvals are invalidated when new commits are pushed, ensuring reviewers always see the latest code.

title: Code owner review not required
description: The branch protection rule does not require a review from a designated code owner, allowing changes to critical paths without expert oversight.
recommendation: Enable the code owner review requirement so changes to paths defined in CODEOWNERS must be approved by the designated owner.

title: Pull request reviews not configured
description: The branch protection rule exists but does not include any pull request review requirements, making it possible to merge without peer review.
recommendation: CRITICAL: Configure PR review requirements including required approver count, stale review dismissal, and code owner reviews.

title: Strict status checks not enabled
description: The required status checks configuration does not enforce that the branch is up-to-date with the base branch before merging, which can allow merging code that has not been tested against the latest state.
recommendation: Enable strict status checks to require branches to be up-to-date before merging.

title: No specific status checks required
description: Required status checks are enabled but no specific checks are listed, which means no CI/CD jobs are required to pass before merging.
recommendation: Configure specific required CI/CD checks (e.g. build, test, lint) that must pass before a pull request can be merged.

title: No required status checks configured
description: The branch protection rule has no status check requirement, allowing pull requests to be merged even if CI/CD jobs fail.
recommendation: Configure CI/CD checks that must pass before any pull request can be merged.

title: Force pushes allowed on protected branch
description: Force pushes are permitted on the protected branch, which allows any authorized user to rewrite history, potentially destroying audit trails and causing data loss for collaborators.
recommendation: CRITICAL: Disable force pushes on all protected branches to prevent history rewriting.

title: Branch deletion allowed on protected branch
description: The protected branch can be deleted by users with write access, risking accidental or malicious deletion of the main branch.
recommendation: Disable branch deletion for all protected branches.

title: Signed commits not required
description: Commits are not required to be cryptographically signed, making it impossible to verify whether commits genuinely came from the stated author.
recommendation: Enable required commit signing to verify the authorship and integrity of every commit merged into the default branch.

title: Linear history not required
description: Merge commits are allowed, which can result in a complex, non-linear commit history that is harder to read and bisect.
recommendation: Consider requiring squash or rebase merges to maintain a clean, linear commit history.

title: Branch protected by repository rulesets (not legacy branch protection)
description: The default branch is not protected by a legacy branch protection rule, but is protected by one or more repository rulesets. Rulesets are the recommended mechanism and provide equivalent or better protection.
recommendation: No action required. Repository rulesets are the modern, recommended approach. Consider migrating any remaining legacy branch protection rules to rulesets for a unified configuration.

title: GitHub Discussions not enabled
description: GitHub Discussions is not enabled for this repository. Discussions provide a structured forum for questions, announcements, and community conversation separate from the issue tracker.
recommendation: Consider enabling GitHub Discussions to provide a dedicated channel for community Q&A and to reduce noise in the issue tracker.

title: Issues and Discussions both disabled
description: Both GitHub Issues and GitHub Discussions are disabled for this repository, leaving contributors with no built-in channel for feedback, bug reports, or questions.
recommendation: Consider enabling Issues or Discussions to provide a structured channel for community feedback and bug reports.

title: Auto-delete branches on merge not enabled
description: Branches are not automatically deleted after a pull request is merged. This leads to accumulation of stale branches over time.
recommendation: Enable auto-delete branch after merge in repository settings to keep the repository clean.

title: Repository has no description
description: The repository has no description set, making it harder for developers to understand its purpose at a glance when browsing the organization.
recommendation: Add a concise description to every repository to improve discoverability and help new contributors understand the project's purpose quickly.

title: Repository has no topics
description: The repository has no GitHub topics assigned. Topics improve searchability and enable org-wide filtering via custom properties or ruleset targeting.
recommendation: Add relevant GitHub topics to this repository to improve searchability and enable org-wide automation based on topics.

title: Repository appears dormant but is not archived
description: No commits have been pushed to this repository in over a year, but it has not been archived. Dormant, unarchived repositories add maintenance burden and may confuse developers about whether the project is still active.
recommendation: Archive inactive repositories to reduce maintenance burden and signal to developers that the project is no longer actively maintained.

title: Dependabot alerts not enabled
description: Dependabot alerts are disabled for this repository. GitHub will not notify you when dependencies have known security vulnerabilities.
recommendation: Enable Dependabot alerts in repository settings to receive security vulnerability notifications for your dependencies.

title: Critical Dependabot alerts open
description: One or more critical-severity Dependabot alerts are currently open in this repository, indicating known critical security vulnerabilities in dependencies.
recommendation: Immediately address critical security vulnerabilities by updating the affected dependencies or applying patches.

title: High-severity Dependabot alerts open
description: One or more high-severity Dependabot alerts are currently open in this repository.
recommendation: Prioritize fixing high-severity dependency vulnerabilities to reduce the attack surface of this repository.

title: No SECURITY.md file found
description: The repository does not have a SECURITY.md file, which means there is no documented process for reporting security vulnerabilities.
recommendation: Add a SECURITY.md file to document how security vulnerabilities should be reported for this project.

title: No CODEOWNERS file found
description: The repository does not have a CODEOWNERS file, so changes to critical paths have no designated reviewer requirement.
recommendation: Add a CODEOWNERS file to assign code ownership and ensure that changes to sensitive paths require review from the appropriate team.

title: Dependabot alerts enabled but no dependabot.yml found
description: Dependabot vulnerability alerts are enabled but there is no .github/dependabot.yml configuration file, so automated dependency update pull requests are not configured.
recommendation: Create a .github/dependabot.yml file to enable automated dependency version update pull requests in addition to alerts.

title: Dependabot not configured
description: Dependabot is not configured for this repository. Neither alerts nor automated dependency updates are enabled.
recommendation: Enable Dependabot alerts and create .github/dependabot.yml for automated security and version updates of dependencies.

title: Code scanning (CodeQL) not configured
description: GitHub code scanning (CodeQL) has not been configured for this repository, leaving it without automated static analysis for security vulnerabilities.
recommendation: Enable GitHub code scanning with CodeQL to automatically detect security vulnerabilities in your source code on every pull request.

title: No custom CodeQL configuration file
description: No custom CodeQL configuration file was found. The default CodeQL configuration may miss some project-specific security queries.
recommendation: Consider creating a custom CodeQL configuration file to extend scanning with additional security queries or to exclude false-positive paths.
